idea-capturer.notes: Deleted SCAMPER/5-Whys/Zettelkasten explanations and full example session. Led with capture-vs-develop-vs-organize decision tree and 'could I forget this?' heuristic. Added mode discipline and idea-type pivot table.

constellation-team.notes: Kept Navigation pattern (80 lines). Added anti-patterns for multi-role orchestration and a disambiguation table distinguishing frontend-pe vs frontend-uiux-designer vs ultrathink-frontend vs frontend-design-excellence within the constellation workflow.

concept-cartographer.notes: Moved mermaid syntax examples to references/mermaid-examples.md (one per type). Kept domain-focus table, topic-inventory verification, max-15-nodes rule. Added 7 non-obvious anti-patterns.

transcript-pipeline.notes: Kept the Navigation skill structure; added a specific Anti-Patterns section covering self-certification drift, segment ledger tampering, unaligned chunk merges, deep-pass override, Colab prompt misuse, premature publish, exceptions.json handling, and ledger edits.

baron-von-markup.notes: Deleted sections restating basic Markdown rules (heading hierarchy, list markers, code-fence basics, ambiguity handling restating intent). Led with the three Baron Standard pillars. Kept emoji palette table, four operating modes table, and strengthened anti-pattern list. Consolidated workflow from 14 to 13 steps.

backend-architecture-standards.notes: Stripped generic best-practice bullets (define SLOs, validate inputs, TLS, least privilege, circuit breakers as category, caching basics). Kept cross-cutting content: RTO/RPO discipline, exactly-once/saga/outbox patterns, migration reversibility, hot-path/batch separation, cross-service data ownership. Cross-referenced backend-pe-{language} and qa-security-engineer.

security-compliance-standards.notes: Stripped generic security best practices (don't hardcode secrets, use TLS, validate inputs, deny by default, rotate tokens). Kept authN protocol decision tree (OIDC/SAML/mTLS/IAP/cloud IAM by context), encryption-at-rest criteria (KMS vs app-level vs HSM vs envelope), audit-log tamper-evidence patterns (append-only, chaining, external anchoring, signing). Cross-referenced qa-security-engineer and backend-architecture-standards.
